2605.24212.title: Distributionally Robust Transfer Learning with Structurally Missing Covariates, with Application to Cross-National Cardiac Arrest Prediction
2605.24212.paper_summary: Deploying clinical prediction models across healthcare systems often fails when key training covariates are unavailable at deployment and labeled outcomes are limited in the target domain. For example, high-performing models for out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) rely on detailed prehospital measurements routinely collected in high-resource settings but unavailable in many international registries. Existing methods either discard missing covariates, sacrificing predictive information, or rely on untestable assumptions about their target distribution. We propose DRUM (\underline{D}istributionally \underline{R}obust \underline{U}nsupervised transfer learning with structurally \underline{M}issing covariates), a framework that transfers prediction models to target populations where certain covariates are structurally absent and outcome labels are unavailable. DRUM partitions covariates into shared components ($X$), observed across all settings, and missing components ($A$), observed only in the source. Rather than imputing missing covariates, DRUM optimizes worst-case predictive performance over the unknown target distribution of $A \mid X$ using a neural network generator, with a robustness parameter controlling allowable deviation from the source conditional. We further develop a bias correction procedure that reduces sensitivity to nuisance estimation error. Simulations show substantial improvements in both mean and worst-case prediction error under distribution shift. Applied to cross-national OHCA prediction, transferring models from a US registry to multiple Asian registries where prehospital variables are unrecorded, DRUM yields better-calibrated predictions and improved clinical classification performance across sites.
2605.24212.paper_summary_zh: 在醫療系統中部署臨床預測模型經常失敗，當關鍵的訓練協變數在部署時不可用，且目標領域的標記結果有限時。例如，針對院外心臟驟停（OHCA）的高效能模型依賴於在高資源環境中常規收集的詳細院前測量，但在許多國際登記中卻不可用。現有的方法要麼丟棄缺失的協變數，犧牲預測信息，要麼依賴於對其目標分佈的無法檢驗的假設。我們提出了DRUM（\underline{D}istributionally \underline{R}obust \underline{U}nsupervised transfer learning with structurally \underline{M}issing covariates），這是一個將預測模型轉移到某些協變數結構性缺失且結果標籤不可用的目標人群的框架。DRUM將協變數劃分為共享組件（$X$），在所有環境中都可觀察到，以及缺失組件（$A$），僅在來源中可觀察到。DRUM並不對缺失的協變數進行插補，而是使用神經網絡生成器優化在未知目標分佈$A \mid X$下的最壞情況預測性能，並使用一個穩健性參數來控制與來源條件的允許偏差。我們進一步開發了一個偏差修正程序，以減少對干擾估計誤差的敏感性。模擬顯示在分佈轉變下，平均和最壞情況預測誤差都有顯著改善。應用於跨國OHCA預測，將模型從美國登記轉移到多個亞洲登記，其中院前變數未被記錄，DRUM產生了更好校準的預測和改善的臨床分類性能。
2605.24212.author: Siqi Li et.al.
2605.24212.authors: Siqi Li, Chuan Hong, Ziye Tian, Benjamin Sieu-Hon Leong, Koshi Nakagawa, Hideharu Tanaka, Sang Do Shin, Khuong Quoc Dai, Do Ngoc Son, Marcus Eng Hock Ong, Nan Liu, Molei Liu
